ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON MAY-08-2007 IN 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR AUG2707 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206; SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR 7/25/07 @ 1:30PM IN DEPT. 303. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TO BE CONCLUDED BY 5/25/07. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 6/8/07, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. DEFENDANTS TO PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING PROPOSITION 51 WITNESSES AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BY 6/28/07. IF DEFENDANTS LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: D. LOK; REPORTER: SUSAN LEE CSR #4280 (206)

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR ON OCT-23-2013 IN 503, DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS RULING - DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED. DEFENDANT SUSTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN. THE DECLARATION OF RUDY LIMPERT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT IS LIABLE FOR MR. SIEGEL'S EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT'S AMMCO BRAKE GRINDING MACHINE. WHILE SUCH MACHINE ITSELF DID NOT CONTAIN ASBESTOS OR ASBESTOS-CONTAINING COMPONENT PARTS, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY TO MR. SIEGEL. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATIONS OF CRAIG MOUNTZ AND RUSSELL DARNELL ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF RUDY LIMPERT ARE OVERRULED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its ruling - Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The declaration of Rudy Limpert creates a triable issue whether defendant is liable for Mr. Siegel's exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant's Ammco brake grinding machine. While such machine itself did not contain asbestos or asbestos-containing component parts, there is a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Siegel. Plaintiffs' objections to the declarations of Craig Mountz and Russell Darnell are overruled. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Rudy Limpert are overruled. Prevailing party to prepare an order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter Not Reported (503/TLJ)

The defendants oppose the motion to consolidate. Defendants must file opposition by tomorrow, 1/11/12. The motion will be heard 1/12/12 at 2:00 p.m.

The deadline for settlement is 1/18/12 at 2:00 p.m. Hardship will be conducted 1/19/12. The statute of limitation deadline for Siegel (456189) is 2/8/12.

At 10:20 a.m., this matter is adjourned.

Petition To Approve Compromise Of Disputed Claim Or Pending Action Or Disposition Of Proceeds Of Judgment For, Karlina Siegel & Laila Siegel, Minor Or Adult Person With A Disability
Off Calendar Apr-07-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the Petitions to Approve the Compromises of the Disputed Claims of Two Minors.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Amir Sarreshtehdary, Esq. appeared for petitioner Sandy Siegel, a/k/a Sandra Siegel the Guardian ad litem for the minors, Karlina Siegel and Laila Siegel.
Neither petitioner nor the minors were present at today's hearing.
Orders waiving witness appearance at the minor's compromise hearing was filed on March 13, 2009.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petitions are granted and compromises are approved.

Settlement Amount as to each of the minors - Karlina Siegel & Laila Siegel - $0.00.
Attorney's Fees as to each of the minors - N/A.
Costs as to each of the minors - N/A.

A net balance of $0.00 shall be deposited into a blocked accounts for each of the minors with Wells Fargo Bank, located at 775 Oak Grove Road, Concord, CA 94518-3672. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order or until the minors reach the age of 18 years.

Furthermore, future settlements from defendants in this action may be distributed in accordance with the terms set forth in Attachment 12 of the Orders Approving the Compromises, without further Order of the Court.

Orders Approving the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor, and to Deposit Money Into a Blocked Account, were submitted to and signed by the Court.
